evidence_quotes.quote: servers, keys and other means to render bought software useless.

paragraphs.text: I am concerned in the trend in software, among which games, to make it dependent on producer/distributor-held required parts to make them work. With this I mean servers, keys and other means to render bought software useless. People buy and pay for a product but find it at some point that it will no longer perform its function because the producer/distributor turned off a part required to make it work. When explicitly, and clearly, stated the thing you buy is not a product but a service this behaviour could, and should, be treated differently, but when someone buys a product it should be up to the buyer to decide to stop using it and producers/distributors should be held to providing a way to make this possible. I thank you.
